items.title: Always enable branch coverage
items.why: Line coverage hides untaken branches; branch coverage is the stricter, more honest signal.
items.example: Put it in `pyproject.toml` via `addopts` so it's the default.

items.title: Read the HTML report
items.prefer: `--cov-report=html` and click through the red lines once a sprint.
items.why: The value isn't the number — it's the conversation 'should this branch be tested?'
items.example: `open htmlcov/index.html` and skim modules with <90% coverage.

items.title: Diff-coverage on PRs
items.prefer: `diff-cover` (or `pytest-cov` + `coverage-diff`) to require N% on changed lines.
items.why: Total-project numbers are sticky; new code is what's actually at risk.

items.title: Per-module thresholds
items.prefer: Critical modules: 90%+ branch. Glue/CLI: 60-70% line. Generated: excluded.
items.why: Different code has different risk; one number is too blunt.
items.example: Use `coverage report --fail-under` per package or a CI script enforcing per-path floors.

items.title: Document every exclusion
items.avoid: `# pragma: no cover` with no comment.
items.why: Otherwise the exclusion outlives its reason; real gaps hide behind 'oh that's just excluded'.
items.example: `# pragma: no cover  -- exercised only in nightly smoke suite`.
